events.text: Assessment complete. The two-leg sling at 45° from horizontal lifting a 6,000 lb load produces ~4,243 lb tension per leg (T = (W/2)/sin45°), which exceeds the tagged 4,000 lb WLL per leg — combined rigging capacity at this angle (5,657 lb) is also below the 6,000 lb load.
